sourcePage.rawText:
5   The curve S has equation


                                      y = px2 + 6x − q

    where p and q are constants.

    S has a line of symmetry at x = − 14 and touches the x-axis at exactly one point.

    What is the value of p + 8q ?

    A 6

    B 18

    C 21

    D 25

    E 38




                                            7

stem.rawText:
The curve S has equation


                                      y = px2 + 6x − q

    where p and q are constants.

    S has a line of symmetry at x = − 14 and touches the x-axis at exactly one point.

    What is the value of p + 8q ?

solution.rawText:
The line of symmetry can be determined by completing the square of the right hand side of the
given equation for S:

                              px2 + 6x − q = p(x2 + p6 x) − q
                                                     3 2    9
                                           =p x+           − 2 −q
                                                      p       p
                                                   3 2 9
                                           =p x+         − −q
                                                   p        p


so the line of symmetry is at x = − p3 . Therefore − p3 = − 41 , giving p = 12.
The curve touches the x-axis at exactly one point, so we must also have − p9 − q = 0, giving
q = − p9 = − 12
             9
                = − 34 .
Therefore p + 8q = 12 − 6 = 6, which is option A.
